items.title: 'Funflation' hits home: Why staying in isn't the cost-saver it used to be
items.body: Following a wave of price hikes, at-home pastimes like streaming movies or playing video games are pinching pocketbooks.

items.title: India's inflation accelerates to 4.38% in June, exceeding forecasts
items.body: Inflation has risen for the eighth straight month in India, as food and energy prices rise due to the Iran war and deficient rainfall.

items.title: Job seekers giving up: Labor force participation rate falls to lowest in 50 years, outside of Covid era
items.body: A drop in the unemployment rate helped provide some upside to what was an otherwise downbeat jobs report — but it was for all the wrong reasons.

items.title: China consumer price growth weakens in June while producer inflation rises to near 4-year high
items.body: Investors increasingly view China's two-speed growth with robust export and tepid domestic demand as a defining long-term feature of the Chinese economy.

items.title: Christine Lagarde leaves door open to early ECB exit, as she mulls French politics
items.body: The European Central Bank's Christine Lagarde has declined to rule out an early end to her term as president, as she mulls a foray into French politics.

items.title: U.S. job creation cools in June with payrolls growth of just 57,000; unemployment rate at 4.2%
items.body: Nonfarm payrolls were expected to rise by 115,000 in June and the unemployment rate to hold steady at 4.3%.

items.title: Europe wants to rebalance trade with Beijing, but can't quit Chinese air conditioners
items.body: A historic heat wave has driven unprecedented demand for Chinese-made air conditioners in Europe, underscoring the tough task Brussels faces in rebalancing trade with Beijing.

items.title: World Cup could boost the June jobs report by 40,000, Goldman estimates
items.body: Nonfarm payrolls are projected to post a gain of 115,000, according to the Dow Jones consensus.
